question: A condition for judicial independence is the appointment of judges by the __________

The Judicial Service Commission is responsible for the appointment, promotion and discipline of judges. This system helps ensure that the judiciary remains independent from the executive and legislative branches.

question: The highest court in Nigeria before 1963 was the _________

The Privy Council served as the final court of appeal for Nigeria during the colonial period and up until the Nigerian Constitution of 1963, when Nigeria established its own Supreme Court as the highest judicial authority in the country.

question: The absence of the rule of law in government will bring about …

question: The Economic Community of West African States has made progress in the area of ....

solution: The Economic Community of West African States has made impressive progress in the area of increased trade among members. ECOWAS has implemented various policies and agreements aimed at boosting intra-regional trade. These include the creation of a free trade area and the adoption of a common external tariff to facilitate the movement of goods and services within the region. These efforts have contributed to increased trade among member states.s

question: The highest grade in the Civil Service is known as the ............
solution:
The Administrative cadre comprises the most senior civil servants who organize and coordinate the activities of the ministries. They execute high-level administrative work, advise ministers in the formulation and implementation of policies and serve as the communication link between the minister and other civil servants.

Recruitment to this class is based on university education and very competitive written examinations and interviews conducted by the civil service commission. In this class are directors-general, other directors, deputy directors, assistant directors, etc.

question: The type of government operated in Nigeria between October 1st 1979 to December 3 1983 is called a __________
solution: During this period, Nigeria operated under the Second Republic, which was modelled after the United States' presidential system. In this system, the President is both the head of state and the head of government, separate from the legislature. This period marked a shift from the parliamentary system used during the First Republic.

question: One of the constitutional requirements for the registration of political parties in Nigeria is ______

option.a: provision for review of party manifesto
option.d: national presence in the federal capital territory

solution: The Party in its formation must provide evidence of ownership or rental of an office in at least 24 States of the Federation, with its headquarters located in the Federal Capital Territory.

question: An electoral system in which the winner must secure more than 50% of the vote is described as a/an
solution: The electoral system in which the winner must secure more than 50% of the vote is described as an "absolute majority," This means the candidate or option needs to obtain more than half of the total votes cast to win.

question: A one-party system helps to prevent
solution: One of the merits of a one-party system is that it helps to prevent economic waste. With only one party in a state, resources do not have to be diverted to conducting elaborate elections in the selection of leaders.

question: A major consequence of the privatization exercise in Nigeria is

option.a: the collapse of the Nigerian economy

solution: The sole aim of Privatization is profit-making. There is often a reduction of costs or spending and  mass retrenchment of labour in response to economic difficulty.

question: One of these is a major problem of the Nigerian civil service
solution: Corruption is widely recognized as one of the major problems of the Nigerian civil service. It manifests in various forms such as embezzlement, bribery, nepotism, and mismanagement of public funds, which significantly hinder efficiency and service delivery.

question: The basic unit of every political organization in the Igbo pre-colonial era was the
solution: The Family Group is one of the most recognized institutions in pre-colonial Igbo society as the basic unit of every political institution.

question: The defence pact between Nigeria and Britain was cancelled in the year
solution: The defence pact between Nigeria and Britain, known as the Anglo-Nigerian Defense Pact, was indeed cancelled in 1962. This agreement, signed in 1960 shortly after Nigeria gained independence from Britain, allowed British military presence and assistance in Nigeria. However, Nigeria sought to assert its sovereignty more fully, leading to negotiations in 1962 that resulted in the cancellation of the pact. This move was part of Nigeria's broader efforts to establish its independence and autonomy in foreign and defence policies following decolonization.

question: The absence of an independent judiciary is a feature of
solution: In an oligarchy, power is concentrated in the hands of a few, and there is often a lack of an independent judiciary to check the power of the ruling group.

question: On 1 October 1995, Abacha announced a three-year program for return to civilian rule. Parties were allowed to form in July 1998. Which of the following parties was not registered by the Provisional Ruling Council?
solution: Three parties were registered by the Provisional Ruling Council for participation in local, state and national elections: the All People's Party or APP led by Mahmud Waziri; the People's Democratic Party or PDP led by Soloman Lar; and the Alliance for Democracy or AD, led by Ayo Adebanjo.

question: Which of the following is a fundamental principle of democratic governance?
solution: Citizen Participation is a fundamental principle of democratic governance. In a democracy, citizens have the right to participate in decision-making processes that affect their lives and their community. This participation can take various forms, including voting in elections, running for office, participating in civic groups, and voicing their opinions through protest and dialogue.

question: In a parliamentary system of government, the head of government is typically ___________.

option.b: the leader of the majority party in the legislature
option.d: chosen by the head of state

solution: In a parliamentary system of government, the head of government is typically the leader of the majority party in the legislature. This is because the head of government, often called the prime minister, is usually the leader of the political party that has the most seats in the legislature.

question: During the cold war, Nigeria's foreign policy was characterized by
solution: During the cold war, Nigeria's foreign policy was characterized by Neutrality and non-alignment. This is because Nigeria did not want to be seen as taking sides in the ideological war between the United States and the Soviet Union. Instead, Nigeria pursued a policy of neutrality and non-alignment, focusing on its own national interests and the promotion of peace and stability in Africa.

question: Which of the following is a right associated with citizenship?
solution: The right to vote is a right associated with citizenship. This is because voting is a fundamental way in which citizens participate in the democratic process. Other rights associated with citizenship include the right to run for public office, the right to work, and the right to receive government services.

question: Which of the following is a function of the Public Complaints Commission?
solution: Public commissions are responsible for conducting investigations and enquiries. They are established to investigate matters of public concern and to make recommendations based on their findings. They do not implement foreign policies, enforce criminal laws, or manage public transportation.

question: n which system of government is the executive and legislature fused?
solution: All members of the executive are part of the legislature.

question: The arm of government responsible for budget approval and oversight is the
solution: The legislative arm of government is responsible for budget approval and oversight. This is because the legislature has the power of the purse, meaning it is responsible for approving the government's budget and overseeing its implementation to ensure that funds are used as intended.

question: The National assembly is composed of how many chambers?
solution: The National Assembly in Nigeria is bicameral, meaning it is composed of two chambers: the Senate and the House of Representatives.

question: Which political party was known for its socialist ideology and populist policies in the 1980s?
solution: The People's Redemption Party was known for its socialist ideology and populist policies in the 1980s. The party was founded by Mallam Aminu Kano and was known for its commitment to representing the interests of the common people.

question: The electoral system used in Nigeria is primarily based on __________
solution: The electoral system used in Nigeria is primarily based on the first-past-the-post system. This means that the candidate with the most votes in an election wins, regardless of whether they have a majority of the total votes. This system is used in Nigeria's presidential, gubernatorial, and legislative elections.

question: Which African country experienced a violent resistance movement against British colonial rule known as the Mau Mau rebellion?
solution: The Mau Mau rebellion was a military conflict that took place in British Kenya between 1952 and 1960. It was a violent resistance movement against British colonial rule. The Mau Mau, a secret society that was predominantly Kikuyu, initiated the conflict with the goal of removing British rule and European settlers from the country.

question: Civil servants are typically protected by
solution: Civil servants are typically protected by the Civil Service Commission. The Civil Service Commission is a government agency that is responsible for the appointment, promotion, and discipline of civil servants.

question: The judiciary checks the other arms of government through
solution: The judiciary checks the other arms of government through judicial review. Judicial review is a process under which executive and legislative actions are subject to review by the judiciary. This is a key aspect of the separation of powers.

question: which pre-colonial Nigerian Kingdom was known for its artistic and cultural achievement?
solution: The Benin Kingdom, in pre-colonial Nigeria, was known for its artistic and cultural achievements. The kingdom was renowned for its brass sculptures, its ivory carvings, and its palace architecture. The Benin Kingdom's art was so highly valued that it influenced the development of modern Western art.

question: The party system that Nigeria operated in the aborted Third Republic is
solution: The aborted Third Republic was the one where Ibrahim Babangida called for a transition to civil rule. The 1989 constitution was initiated and it established a two-party system.

question: Which of the following is a basic feature of the rule of law?
solution:
The basic features rule of the law are:

1. Equality before the law

2. Impartiality

3. Individual rights

4. Supremacy of the law

5. Fair hearing, etc.

In a country that upholds the rule of law, everyone is equal before the law. This means that everyone is treated fairly regardless of political, social or economic status. No body is above the law and everyone has equal access to all legal facilities.

question: The productivity of public corporations is believed to be enhanced when there is
solution: Public Corporation operates better when there is little to no political interference. This is because it allows the corporation to function like a business rather than a government body, reduce corruption and improve accountability. Measures such as privatisation, commercialisation and deregulation can be taken to reduce political interference

question: Nigerian Ports Authority is an example of
solution: The Nigerian Port Authority is an example of a public corporation. A public corporation is a government organisation that provides essential services to the public. The Nigerian Ports Authority is responsible for providing smooth unloading and offloading of exported and imported cargo.

question: The commander of the army under the Hausa/ Fulani pre-colonial administration was
solution: The madawaki was the commander of the army in Pre-colonial Hausa/ Fulani Empire.

question: The rationale behind the adoption of a federal system of government in Nigeria is to
solution: The major reason for the adoption of the Federal system of Government was to ensure unity despite the cultural, religious and ethnic diversity prevalent in Nigeria. Each state of the Federation gets to create and implement laws and policies that are specific to the region while still reporting to the central government. This helps to maintain unity despite the diversity in the country.

question: Shehu Shagari won the 1979 Presidential Election under
solution: Shehu Shagari won the 1979 Presidential Election under the umbrella of the National Party of Nigeria (NPN). It also who the 1983 Presidential Election which did not take effect because of the transition to military rule.

question: As a non-aligned state, Nigeria attended the second meeting of the non-Aligned Movement that took place in Cairo, Egypt in
solution: Nigeria attended the 2nd Summit of the Non-Aligned Movement hosted by the United Arab Emirates in Cairo, Egypt. It took place between 5th to 10th October, 1964.

question: The power of prerogative of mercy rests with the
solution: The "prerogative of mercy" is one of the powers of the executive. It is also a method of checking the activities of the Judiciary. It is the power of the president to grant pardon to a convicted criminal or to commute his/her sentence.

question: ECOWAS was established in
solution: The Economic Community of West African States (ECOWAS) was established on the 28th of May, 1975 under the regime of General Yakubu Gowon. It has its administrative headquarters in Abuja, Nigeria and its financial headquarters in Lome, Togo.

question: The rule of law is an essential ingredient of
solution: The rule of law is an essential ingredient of democracy. Considering that democracy is a government of the people, by the people and for the people, it is imperative that every person within this system is treated equally and fairly. This equality is assured when the rule of law is practiced (the rule that states that no body is above the law/ every one is equal before the law).

question: The electoral process in a representative government does NOT include
solution:
One of the fundamental necessities in an electoral process is a free and fair election. An election should be free and fair in the sense that political parties should be allowed to contest without prejudice and citizens should be allowed to vote the leaders of their choice without undue coercion. The freedom of the press is also essential. They should be allowed to inform the public about the happenings during the election without undue censorship. The Electoral Commission should also work independently of the government to ensure a free and fair election. 

However, registration of births is not essential to the electoral process. Registration of voters, on the other hand, in necessary.

question: The Commission that examines complaints of civil wrongs committed by government employees is
solution: The Public Complaints Commission is a body responsible for receiving an responding to complaints from the public about the wrongdoings of civil servants and other government employees. They have the power to offer recommendations for the punishments of such employees.

question: Lack of separation of power is a feature of
solution: Separation of power is the division of powers and functions among the three arms of government - the legislature, executive and judiciary. This is essential to prevent the abuse of power when it is concentrated in the hands of a single authority. In a presidential system, there is a clear separation of power. In a cabinet system, there is still separation of power although, it is not as clear (this is because the legislature and executive arms are fused). However, in a military government, power is concentrated in the hands of the military leader. He has the power to make laws through the promulgation of decrees; to execute the law; as well as to punish law breakers. In this case, there is a lack of separation of power.

question: Which local government reform in Nigeria prevented traditional rulers from engaging in politics?
solution: The 1976 Reform of the Local Government in Nigeria changed the multiple system of local government councils to a single-tier all-purpose local government. It also led to the exclusion of traditional rulers from local government councils.

question: A union of states with a common recognised authority is
solution: A federal system is a system whereby power is shared among the federal level and the component units. The component units (the states) report to a common authority at the centre (the federal level).

question: The fragmented nature of the Igbo pre-colonial political system qualified it to be described as
solution: The Igbo pre-colonial political system is described as fragmented because there is no centralised political authority. This makes the Igbo society acephalous (without a head) in nature.

question: The most effective instrument adopted by Nigeria against the East-West Cold War in the 1960s was
solution: During the 1960s, Nigeria adopted the non-alignment policy as an effective instrument to navigate the East-West Cold War. This policy meant that Nigeria chose not to align with either the capitalist bloc (led by the United States) or the communist bloc (led by the Soviet Union). Instead, it focused on maintaining independence in foreign relations and avoiding entanglement in ideological conflicts between the two superpowers.

question: The standing committee of a legislature is one
solution: Standing committees are permanent bodies established by statute or by the rules of the legislature. Their duties include reviewing proposed legislation, overseeing government functions, and making recommendations based on clearly defined legal responsibilities. This statutory framework is what distinguishes them from temporary or ad hoc committees.

question: The fundamental rights of citizens include rights to

option.a: free education, employment and freedom of thought

solution: Fundamental rights typically include the right to life, freedom of speech, and freedom of association. These rights ensure that citizens can live freely, express themselves openly, and gather with others, which are essential components of a democratic society.

question: Public opinion is a view that is
solution:
Public opinion is a view that is held by the majority. As far as public opinion is concerned, the view must be held and expressed by the majority of the society.

According to G. A. I. Ofoegbu (1999), public opinion is the believes, values and attitudes which are commonly held and expressed by majority of the people on a given issue.

question: The method used by the British to facilitate the administration of Southern Nigeria was
solution:
The method used by the British to facilitate the administration of Southern Nigeria was divide and rule.
The system brought about division between the traditional rulers who were looked upon a British puppets and the alienated educated elites thereby, strengthening the policy divide and rule.

question: A major function of the warrant chiefs was to
solution:
A major function of the warrant Chiefs was to take charge of local administration.

The chiefs were the heads of the administration of their various communities.

question: When Nigeria achieved independence in 1960, the head of state was the
solution:
When Nigeria achieved independence in 1960, the Head of state was the Governor-General, representing the queen of England.
One of the main provisions of the constitution of 1960 was that the governor general was made the constitutional ceremonial Head of state and a representative of the Queen of England.

This means the queen was the head of state but appointed a representative in the person of the governor-general to stand in for her, in her absence.

question: Which of these constitutions recognized local government as a third tier of government in Nigeria?
solution: The 1979 constitutions recognized local government as a third tier government in Nigeria. General Murtala Mahammed inaugurated a Constitution Drafting Committee (CDC)on October 18, 1975. The draft constitution was then approved by General Olusegun Obasanjo as the 1979 Nigeria cobnstitution which took effect from October 1, 1979. One of the features of the constitution is that it provided for democratically elected local government councils as the third tier of government in Nigerias

question: The equivalent of the commissioner at the local government level is the
solution: The equivalent of a commissioner at the local government level is the supervisory Councillor. At the state level, a commissioner is the highest or completely in-charge of a particular ministry. For instance, the commissioner for education. He does not come in through election but through the appointment by the government of the state. Coming to the local government level, the supervisory Councillor for education as well as any other supervisory Councillor is appointed to the Executive Chairman of the local government subject to the approved of the Councillors.

question: The Independent National Electoral Commission has the power to prepare and maintain the register of
solution: The Independent National Electoral Commission has the power to prepare and maintain the register of voters. One of functions of the body is registration of voters, compilation of voters register and updating of same from time to time.

question: The land use decree of 1978 vested the ownership of land in Nigeria in the
solution: The Land Use Decree of 1978 (later revised as the Land Use Act) vested the ownership of land in Nigeria's state governments. According to the decree, all land in urban and rural areas is held in trust by the state governors, who are responsible for managing it on behalf of the citizens. The goal was to ensure better land administration and allocation across the country.

question: South-South cooperation is a major policy plan on which Nigeria bases her relations with
solution: South-South cooperation refers to the collaboration between countries in the Global South, primarily focused on mutual development, technical exchange and sharing of resources and knowledge to address common challenges. Nigeria, as a developing country, engages in South-South cooperation with other developing nations, rather than with developed countries.

question: The first Nigerian Permanent Representative to the United Nations was
solution:
Among the four listed options, Chief Simeon Adebo served earliest as Nigeria’s Permanent Representative to the United Nations. He held this position from 1962 to 1967, which is before the tenures of Alhaji Yusuf Maitama Sule (1979–1983), General Joseph Garba (1984–1989), and Professor Ibrahim Gambari (1990–1999).

Although Jaja Wachuku (1960–1961) and Muhammad Ngileruma (1961–1962) served before Adebo, they are not among the answer choices, making Chief Adebo the first among the listed options.

question: Nigeria's withdrawal from the Edinburgh Commonwealth Games in July 1986 was in protest against British

option.a: support for UNITA rebels in Angola
option.c: failure to impose sanctions on South Africa

solution:
Nigeria's withdrawal from the Edinburgh Commonwealth Games in July 1986 was in protest against British failure to impose sanctions on South Africa.


She, Britain did not support but in Apartheid regime in South Africa.

question: Communism is a system which recognizes
solution:
Communism is a system which recognizes the existence of the state.

This is so as communism may be defined as an economic system and a system of government where the means of production, distribution and exchange are totally controlled by the state.

Some features of communism are as follows:

1. Total ownership of means of production and distribution, by the state.

2. Absence of classes, ie classes society.

3. Centrally planned economy.

4. Authoritarian and Totalitarian government

question: The absence of the rule of law in government will bring about
solution:
The absence of the rule of law in government will bring about human rights abuse.

This is so as the rule of law refers to the constitutional provision which emphasizes the supremacy of the law, the equality before the law and existence of fundamental human rights.

question: Under the 1999 Constitution of Nigeria, the power to create local governments is vested in the
solution:
Under the 1999 Constitution of Nigeria, the power to create local governments is vested in the state assembly.

The Constitution made it clear that it is the duty of the state assembly to pass the bill for the creation of new local governments other duties are conduct of referendum as well as approving the result of the referendum.

question: The main duty of the Local Government Service is to

option.a: supervise and manage the affairs of local governments
option.b: create job opportunities at the local level
option.c: handle requests for the creation of more local governments

solution:
The main duty of the local Government Service Commission is to supervise and manage the affairs of local governments.

The Local Government Service Commission was established in each state of the Federation in 1976 to control all services in the local governments e.g recruitment, promotion, discipline etc. of staff of the local governments.

question: The constitution that introduced restricted franchise into Nigerian politics was the
solution:
Clifford Constitution was the constitution that introduced restricted franchise into Nigerian politics.

The elective principle was restricted to Lagos and Calabar only. Secondly, it was based on limited franchise of 12 months residential permit and $100.00 per annum.

question: In Nigeria, the Council of State was first created by
solution: The Council of State in Nigeria was first created by General Murtala Muhammed. In a broadcast on 30 July 1975—shortly after deposing General Yakubu Gowon—he introduced the idea and structure of the National Council of State. This body was established to advise the executive on various national issues and has evolved over time through constitutional reforms.

question: The leading agent in the expansion of British influence in Northern Nigeria in the 1890s was the
solution:
The leading agent in the expansion of British influence in Northern Nigeria in the 1890s was the Royal Niger Company.

United African Company was formed in 1879 followed by the National African Company. This trading company helped the Emir of Nupe to deal with his rebels. In July 1886, a charter was granted to the company and the name was changed to the Royal Niger company with head quarters at Lokoja.

The Company also served the purpose of developing backward areas in the North as well.

question: The abolition of the state ministries of local governments in 1989 entails that local governments

option.b: have more control over their funds
option.c: no longer have anything to do with state governments
option.d: are no longer surbodinates to state governments

solution:
The abolition of the state ministries of local governments in 1989 entails that local governments are no longer subordinate to state governments.

Previously local governments in Nigeria were under the ministry of government. This means that all their activities were controlled by the ministries of Local Government. With this new development the local government is now a full fledged third tier of government. They can now take actions on any issue without reference to the ministry of local government.

question: The United Nations charter is an instrument that

option.a: sets out the rights and obligations of member states
option.b: provides for funding of memeber states
option.c: facilitates the resolution of disputes among member states
option.d: determines the admission of member states

solution:
The United Nations charter is an instrument that sets out the rights and obligations of member states.

It should be recalled that one of the aims and objectives of U.N.O is to promote respect for Fundamental Human Rights among Nations.

question: The group of states that conceived the idea of the United Nations Organization included

option.a: the USSR, Italy, the UK and China
option.b: Italy, the UK, France and Japan
option.c: the USA, the USSR, the UK and China

solution:
The draft of the U.N.O was prepared under the auspices of the United Kingdom, United States of America, Soviet Union and China.

So the group of states that conceived the idea of the United Nations Organization included the U.S.A, the USSR. the UK and China.

question: The greatest criticism of the Security Council of the UNO is that it
solution: One of the most significant criticisms of the UN Security Council is that its structure gives the five permanent members (the United States, the United Kingdom, France, Russia, and China) the power to veto any substantive resolution. This "exclusive veto power" can block action even when there is broad international support for a measure, which many argue hampers the Council's ability to respond effectively to international crises and conflicts. Critics contend that this arrangement often leads to inaction and prevents the Security Council from reflecting the current global political landscape.

question: Professor Ibrahim Gambari is the Special Assistant to the United Nations Secretary General on
solution: Professor Ibrahim Gambari served as the Under-Secretary-General and Special Adviser on Africa to the United Nations Secretary-General.

question: Who among the following served as Secretary General of OPEC?
solution: Rilwanu Lukman, a Nigerian engineer and technocrat, served as the Secretary General of OPEC from 1995 to 2000. During his tenure, he played a crucial role in managing the organization’s policies and navigating the complexities of global oil markets. His leadership is often noted for its contributions to stabilizing oil production levels and addressing the challenges faced by member countries.

question: Nigeria's active role in the liberation of some countries in Southern Africa earned her

option.a: the chairmanship of the Eminent Persons Group
option.c: recognition as the giant of Africa
option.d: the status of a frontline state

solution:
Nigeria's active role in the liberation of some countries in Southern Africa earned her the status of a frontline state.

It made Nigeria to because very ifluential in the world community as outside countries began to consult Nigeria on issues concerning Africa.

It was also in recognition of Nigeria's contributions to liberation Movements that made ANC deputy President Dr.Nelson Mandela to make Nigeria his first port of call outside the front line states immediately after his release from prison in 1990.

question: Removal by impeachment applies to the position of a
solution:
Removal by impeachment applies to the position of a president.

Impeachment means the process by which the chief executive is removed from office by the parliament.

question: One of the duties of the legislature is to
solution: One of the key responsibilities of the legislature in any democratic system is to exercise oversight over the executive branch and government agencies. This oversight function includes scrutinizing policies, monitoring the implementation of laws, and ensuring accountability in government operations. The legislature does not implement laws (that is the role of the executive), adjudicate disputes (that is the role of the judiciary), or promulgate decrees (also typically an executive function).

question: A common feature of government is
solution:
A common feature of government is the making of public policy.

Government is made up of three arms the legislature, the executive and judiciary. The main function of the legislature is law making while that of the executive is formulation and implementation of policies

question: The best form of government for a heterogeneous society is a
solution:
The best form of government for a heterogeneous society is a federal system.

It should also be noted that heterogeneity, different languages, customs and levels of political economic and social developments among the tribes play a prominent role in forcing confederal system of government on the people.

question: Constitutional disputes in states with written constitutions are resolved by
solution:
Constitutional disputes in a state with written constitution are resolved by the Judiciary.

One of the main functions of the judiciary is to interpret the laws of the land in courts.

It settles disputes between the executive and the legislature; between individuals and the state, etc.

The judiciary interprets and defends the constitution. It has also full power to declare any action unconstitutional if such action goes against the dictates of the constitution especially in a state with written constitution.

question: Public opinion becomes politically relevant when it
solution: Public opinion becomes politically relevant when it actively shapes and influences the decisions made by the government. While public opinion can aggregate views, criticize those in power, or show support, its true political relevance is demonstrated when it translates into pressure on policymakers and affects policy outcomes. This influence on government decisions is key to the functioning of a democratic system, as it holds leaders accountable and ensures that public concerns are reflected in governance.

question: In the Pre-colonial Igbo society,the maintenance of peace and order was,the function of the
solution: In pre-colonial Igbo society, age grades were groups of people of similar ages who played a key role in maintaining peace and order within the community. They were responsible for duties such as defending the community against external aggression, performing police functions, and ensuring that laws and policies were enforced. Their role helped check abuses by other authorities and facilitated the execution of decisions made by community leaders.

question: The Eastern and Western regions of Nigeria achieved the status of self-government is
solution:
The Eastern and Western regions of Nigeria achieved the status of self-government in 1957.

Western Region: Attained self-government on August 8, 1957, under the leadership of Chief Obafemi Awolowo.

Eastern Region: Followed shortly after, gaining self-government on October 1, 1957, led by Dr. Nnamdi Azikiwe.

This milestone was part of Nigeria's gradual decolonization process under British rule, culminating in the country's full independence on October 1, 1960. The Northern Region achieved self-government later, in 1959, due to its more cautious approach to political reforms.

question: One of the legacies of pre-colonial Nigeria destroyed by the British was the

option.a: peace and harmony in the land

solution:
One of the legacies of pre-colonial Nigeria destroyed by the British was the indigenous cultures of the people.

When British people first came to Nigeria, they saw and regarded many aspects of the peoples' way of life as crude and ungodly. They then destroyed and replaced them with their own. Such were religion, mode of dressing, etc.

question: The 1979 Constitution established the
solution: The 1979 Constitution established the National Population Commission

question: The most important challenge facing the Fourth Republic is

option.a: the need to develop Nigeria culturally
option.b: the successful conduct of the 2003 election
option.c: how to develop Nigeria's social institutions
option.d: how to deal with the military

solution:
The most important challenge facing the Fourth Republic is the successful conduct of the 2003 elections.

If certain things are not well done, such issues as rigging and election violence may plunge the nation into state of anarchy and this may eventually attract another military regime.

question: A problem of Nigeria federalism that was resolved by the supreme court between the federal government and littoral states centered on resource
solution: A problem of Nigeria federalism that was resolved by the supreme court between the federal government and littoral states centered on resource control

question: One of the reasons advanced for the overthrow of the Gowon Regime was its failure to

option.a: honour the promise to hand over power
option.b: promote some officers by the armed forces

solution: General Yakubu Gowon's regime (1966–1975) faced criticism for repeatedly postponing its commitment to transition Nigeria back to civilian rule. Initially pledging to hand over power by 1976, Gowon's failure to outline a concrete timeline or take credible steps toward democratization fueled discontent within the military and public. This broken promise, combined with allegations of corruption and economic mismanagement, led to his overthrow in a bloodless coup in 1975 by General Murtala Mohammed.

question: The 1976 Reforms have been most beneficial to the
solution: The 1976 Local Government Reforms in Nigeria were a landmark initiative aimed at restructuring and empowering local governance. These reforms established local governments as the third tier of Nigeria's federal system, granting them constitutional recognition, defined boundaries, and specific administrative and fiscal responsibilities

question: The dispute between Nigeria and Cameroon is over
solution:
The dispute between Nigeria and Cameroon is over territory.

The people of Cameroun have been calming that Bakasi pennisula belongs to them. But Nigeria strongly maintains that the pennisula is one of her territories.

question: Nigeria's high standing in the UN General Assembly is underscored by her
solution:
Nigeria's high standing in the UN General Assembly is underscored by the contributions to global peace.

Nigeria has been contributing much financially, morally, etc, to the liberation movements which is one of the main principles of the United Nations Organization. For instance, Michael Olisa et el (1991) pointed out that the prominent role which Nigeria played in the United Nations from the 1970s brought about its permanent representation in the UN. In addition, Leslie Harriman was appointed the chairman of the special committee against apartheid.

Nigeria has also played prominent and satisfactory roles in conflict resolution.

question: The tenure of the President of the UN Security Council is
solution: The President of the United Nations Security Council serves a tenure of one month, with the position rotating among the 15 member states (5 permanent and 10 non-permanent) in alphabetical order (based on English country names). This monthly rotation ensures equitable representation and shared leadership.

question: With the admission of Asian and African countries to the Commonwealth, the Queen of England became the

option.b: head of state of these countries
option.d: head of government of these countries

solution: The Queen of England became the chairperson of the Commonwealth With the admission of Asian and African countries to the Commonwealth.

question: The Lagos Treaty of May 28th 1975 led to the formation of the

option.c: Economic Community of West African States

solution: The Lagos Treaty of 28 May 1975 (officially the Treaty of Lagos) established the Economic Community of West African States (ECOWAS). This regional bloc was created to promote economic integration, cooperation, and stability among West African nations.

question: The two-chambers of elected national representatives in Nigeria are called
solution: The two-chambers of elected national representatives in Nigeria are called the National Assembly. The chambers are House of Representatives and House of Senate.

question: The pre-colonial Yoruba political system as a whole can best be described as a
solution: The pre-colonial Yoruba political system as a whole can best be described as confederation of monarchs and chiefdoms

question: Under the 1999 Constitution, the power to declare war is vested in the
solution: Under the 1999 Constitution, the power to declare war is vested in the Executive. This is with the approval of the National Assembly.

question: The major motivation for British colonization of Nigeria was to
solution: The major motivation for British colonization of Nigeria was to satisfy British economic interest. They wanted a market for their raw and finished products. British did not allow her colonies to trade directly with other countries. Before Nigeria's independence, Britain spelt out the conditions of trade. For instance she determined the prices of Nigerian import and export commodities.

question: The first franchise in the history of the democratic process is
solution:
The first franchise in the history of the democratic process is male franchise. 
This  is a form of voting rights in which all adult males within a political system are allowed to vote, regardless of income, property, religion, race, or any other qualification.

question: The principle of separation of powers was made popular by
solution: The principle of separation of powers was made popular by Baron Montesquieu. He was a french political thinker and Jurist. In his book titled The Spirit of Law, he divided governmental powers into three separate and co-ordinate branches the legislature, Executive and Judiciary.

question: A major influence on the formation of public opinion is
solution: Mass media—such as television, radio, newspapers, and social media—plays a crucial role in shaping public opinion by framing issues, setting the public agenda, and influencing the way people think about events and policies. It reaches a wide audience and can reinforce or challenge perceptions, making it a major influence on how opinions are formed.

question: The law of libel limits a citizen's right to freedom of
solution:
The law of libel limits a citizen's right to freedom of expression.

Others are the laws of slander and sedition

question: In a unitary system of government,power is concentrated at the center
solution: In a unitary system of government,power is concentrated at the center with residual functions. Some examples of residual lists are markets and chieftancy affairs.

question: The agent through which the state undertakes political socialization is the
solution: The school is the primary agent through which the state deliberately undertakes political socialization. As a state-controlled institution, schools systematically teach civic values, national history, and governance structures via curricula, textbooks, and activities like assemblies or patriotic events. For example, subjects like civics education or national pledge recitations directly instill loyalty to the state and its political norms.

question: Unicameral legislature is a common feature of
solution: A unicameral legislature (single legislative chamber) is most commonly associated with unitary systems of government. In unitary states, power is centralized, and subnational units (if they exist) derive authority from the central government. A single legislative body aligns with this simplicity and centralized decision-making.

question: In a constitutional monarchy,the authority to remove the head of state is exercised by the
solution: In a constitutional monarchy, the authority to remove the head of state is exercised by the legislature

question: One of the sources of a constitution is
solution: One of the sources of a constitution is common law, which consists of legal principles developed through judicial decisions and precedents over time. Many modern constitutions, including Nigeria’s, draw from common law traditions, especially in areas where statutes do not provide clear guidance.

question: Independence of the judiciary is pertinent because it accords the judiciary the power to

option.a: dismiss any judge who has breached the judicial code of conduct
option.b: determine a fixed salary for judges
option.c: determine a fixed term of office for the judges
option.d: enable the judges to try and decide cases without bias

solution: The independence of the judiciary ensures that judges can adjudicate cases impartially, free from external pressures (e.g., political interference, public opinion, or personal interests). This autonomy is critical to upholding the rule of law, protecting individual rights, and maintaining public trust in legal systems.

question: In a democracy,sovereign authority is exercised by the
solution: In a democracy, sovereign authority is exercised by the electorate

question: The 1976 Local Government Reforms in Nigeria transformed the relationship between states and local governments into one of
solution: The 1976 Local Government Reforms in Nigeria transformed the relationship between states and local governments into one of partnership and co-operation

question: The difference between commercialized and privatized companies is that in the former
solution: The difference between commercialized and privatized companies is that in the former public ownership is dominant

question: Nigeria supports the South-South cooperation as a foreign policy objective for mutual understanding among
solution: South-South cooperation refers to collaboration among developing countries, particularly in Africa, Asia, and Latin America, to promote economic and political solidarity. Nigeria supports this as part of its foreign policy to foster economic growth, trade and mutual understanding among developing nations rather than just those south of the equator or limited to Sub-Saharan Africa.

question: Which of the following served as the Secretary-General of the United Nations?
solution:
Javier Perez de Cuellar of Peru served as the Secretary-General of the United Nations Organization from 1982-1992.

Others were:

Trygie Lie of Norway (1946-53), Dag Hammarskjold of Sweden (1953-61), U. Thant of Burma (1961-72), Kurt Waldeim of Austria (1972-82), Boutros Ghali of Egypt (1993-93). The current Secretary General of the Organization is Dr. Koffi Annan of Ghana.

question: Under the emirate system,legislative functions were performed by the
solution: The Emir performed the legislative functions under the emirate system. It should be understood that the laws of emirate were the Islamic or Sharia laws. Where the laws were clear they were to be enforced. But where the Islamic laws were silent. Emirs had power to make laws to provide for peace, orderliness and good government.

question: In the First Republic,the official opposition party was the

option.d: National Council of Nigeria and the Cameroons

solution: The official opposition party in the First Republic was the Action Group.

question: On the basis of its structure,a political party can be classified as indirect if

option.a: its membership is acquired through other groups
option.c: it campaigns for votes through agents

solution: On the basis of its structure,a political party can be classified as indirect if its membership is acquired through other groups

question: How does the president relieve a minister of his appointment in a presidential system of government
solution:
In a presidential system of government, the President relieves a Minister of his appointment by unilateral action.

Going through the powers and functions of the President, it should be observed that the President has the right to dismiss any member of his cabinet and can also reshuffle the cabinet.

question: The study of government essentially facilitates the understanding of the

option.a: functioning of the entire social formation
option.b: organization of the executive arm of government

solution: The study of government will shield  us from ignorance towards the body governing our country. It is really important for anyone to know, how his/her country is being managed. Of course, when we know a lot about the systems governing our country, then, we will also know our responsibilities as a citizen of that country, enlightens citizens on their basic fundamental human rights and avoid the abuse of such rights.

question: The ultimate authority in a state is
solution: Sovereignty is the ultimate authority in a state. It should be noted that sovereignty is absolute power of the state to exercise supreme legal authority over its own affairs within its territory without any form of external control.

question: A major flaw in a liberal democracy is

option.a: the limitation of the freedom of expression
option.b: its emphasis on political rights over economic rights

solution:
A major flaw in a liberal democracy is political instability.

Aristotle even maintained that the real democracy at time give rise to mob rule. It was also breeds constant disagreement in state matters. Such disagreements often result to instability in government.

question: During the Second Republic, the Great Nigerian People's Party won the governorship election in
solution: During the Second Republic, the Great Nigerian People's Party won the governorship election in Borno and Gongola States

question: In the present local government structure ,traditional rulers play
solution:
The traditional rulers play advisory role in the present local government structure.

Since the 1976 local government reform, the traditional rulers have maintained the position of advicers to local government councils. And this is mostly on traditional matters.

question: A key characteristics of the civil service is
solution:
A key characteristic of the civil service is Merit System.

Recruitment into the Civil Service should be based on merit and not on quota or favouritism.

This is to enable the Civil Servants to perform their functions very well and then ensure the success of the government.

question: An example of a country that operates a bicameral legislature is
solution:
Nigeria operates a bicameral legislature composed of two chambers:

Senate (Upper House): 109 members representing the 36 states and the Federal Capital Territory.

House of Representatives (Lower House): 360 members elected from constituencies nationwide.

question: In the presidential system of government , the leader is basically the head of state and
solution:
In the presidential system of government , the leader is basically the head of state and government.

The Presidential system of government is the type of political organization in which the leader popularly known as the President is the head of state in reality. In short, the President is both head of state and that of government. He then combines both the executive and ceremonial functions. He comes into power through free and fair periodic election.

question: The act of denying an eligible person the right to vote in an election is
solution:
Disenfranchisement The act of denying an eligible person the right to vote in an election.

Disenfranchisement means depriving individuals the right to vote at election or the right of citizenship.

question: In the monarchical system of government, sovereignty is vested in the
solution: In a monarchical system, the ultimate authority or sovereignty is traditionally vested in the royal family—specifically, the monarch. Although modern constitutional monarchies often have democratic elements, the traditional principle of monarchy holds that the crown is the source of sovereign power, rather than the people, a legislature, or the military.

question: The first military coup in Nigeria toppled the government of
solution: The first military coup in Nigeria occurred on January 15, 1966, led by a group of army officers (including Major Chukwuma Nzeogwu). This coup overthrew the First Republic civilian government headed by Prime Minister Sir Abubakar Tafewa Balewa, who was killed during the coup.

question: Prior to 1966,the system of local government in western region was based on the three-tier structure of

option.a: native authority, division and district councils

solution: Prior to 1966, the system of local government in western region was based on the three-tier structure of Division, District and Local Councils. In Western Nigeria, there was the 1952 ordinance which established three tier system---divisional, district and local councils. Each tier was given responsibilities according to its resources, with the divisional councils shouldering the greatest responsibilities.

question: A major shortcoming of the Lyttelton Constitution was that

option.a: the Governor-General continued to head the government
option.b: no provision was made for the office of the prime minister
option.c: it failed to provide for the office of a premier
option.d: A major shortcoming of the Lyttelton Constitution was that

solution: The Lyttleton Constitution of 1954, while introducing Nigeria’s first federal structure, faced criticism for failing to establish the office of a Prime Minister at the federal level. Instead, executive authority remained vested in the Governor-General, who chaired the Federal Executive Council and retained veto powers over legislation

question: Which of the following in the Sokoto Caliphate performed functions similar to that of the Bashorun in Oyo Kingdom
solution:
Waziri in the Sokoto Caliphate performed functions similar to that of the Bashorum in Oyo Kingdom.

In the Sokoto Caliphate , Waziri is the senior official, the Emir's administrative adviser who acted as the Prime Minister. In Oyo Kingdom, the Bashorum was the Prime Minister of the empire

question: The arm of government mainly responsible for the implementing of law in Nigeria is the
solution:
The executive arm of government in Nigeria is responsible for implementing and enforcing laws. This includes:

Administrative Action: Ministries, agencies, and public servants (e.g., the police, immigration, customs) execute policies and laws enacted by the legislature.

Law Enforcement: Agencies like the Nigeria Police Force operate under the executive to maintain public order and ensure compliance with laws.

question: The Inspector General of Police is appointed by the executive on the advice of the
solution:
The Inspector General of Police (IGP) in Nigeria is appointed by the President (executive) on the advice of the Nigerian Police Council. This council, established under Section 153 of the 1999 Constitution (as amended), includes:

The President (Chairman)

State Governors

The IGP

The Chairman of the Police Service Commission.

question: A major problem associated with the Nigerian federalism is
solution: A major problem associated with the Nigerian federalism is ethnic conflicts. There has been inter ethnic contact in the various regions but without discrimination and bitter experiences. The issue of neutralization of Lagos in 1953 would have strongly made Chief Obafemi Awolowo to lead the Yorubas to secede from Nigeria but for the threat of war from the Governor and the Colonial office in London. The real secession which was experienced in Nigeria was that of the then Eastern Region in the year 1967. The secession was as a result of the 1966 military coup which was followed by the pogrom that was perpetrated against the Igbos in the North. The East declared the Republic of Biafra and engaged Nigeria in a civil war from 1967-1970. This question of ethnic conflicts has also given rise to other serious issues such has unending demand for creating more states and acceptable revenue allocation formula.

question: The six African members of the Nigerian Council were nominated by the
solution: The six African members of the Nigerian Council were nominated by the governors. The Council was enlarged after the amalgamation so as to cope with the expected increase in problems resulting from increased territory. It was made up of 36 members consisting of the Governor, members of the Executive Council, First Class Residents, Political Secretaries of Northern and Southern provinces as officials. Europeans representing such interests as chambers of Commerce, shipping, banking, mining and six Nigerians nominated by the governor.

question: In the Igbo political system, the most senior member of the council of elders is the
solution:
In the Igbo political system, the most senior member of the council of elders is the Okpara. The Igbo political system was based on the village as a political unit.

A village is then seen as made up of a group of related families. Each family head held the Ofo title and all of them put together formed the council of elders. The council of elders governed the village. Among the Ofo title holders, one of them was regarded as the most senior to as the Okpara and he had the right to preside over the council of elders meetings.

question: The participation of Nigerians in the Second World War created in the nationalists the awareness that the

option.b: Africans were more qualified to rule
option.d: Europeans were less tactful in warfare

solution:
The Participation of Nigerians in the Second World War created in the nationalists the awareness that the Europeans were not superior.

Their participation in the war, made them to know more about the white people, and realized that they were in no way superior to Africans including Nigerians.

question: The power to nominate members of the judiciary at the federal level under the 1999 Constitution is vested in the
solution: Under Nigeria’s 1999 Constitution, the National Judicial Council (NJC) is vested with the authority to nominate members of the federal judiciary for appointment by the President. This process ensures judicial independence and adherence to constitutional standards.

question: Prior to 1976, the local government system practiced in Northern Nigeria was
solution: Prior to 1976, the Local government system practised in Northern Nigerian Nigeria was Chief-in-Council.By 1952, the Northern region abolished the sole Native Authority and put in its place, Chief-in-Council and Chief-and-Council and. The democratization process in the Northern region was further aided by the Native Authority Law of 1954 which was amended by the Northern Nigeria Law of 1963. They provided for five Native Authorities including Chief-in-Council and the Federal Native Authorities.

question: In the segmented Tiv society, governance was usually confined to
solution: In the segmented Tiv society,governance was usually confined to chiefdom . The Tivs were formally traditional worshipers, but some are now Christians. They are ruled by their Chief called Tor Tiv.

question: Which of the following influenced nationalist activities in Nigeria?

option.d: National Congress of British West Africa

solution: In the history of Nigerian nationalism, regional organizations played a significant role in mobilizing and uniting educated elites and activists against colonial rule. The National Congress of British West Africa was one such organization that brought together nationalist leaders from across the British West African colonies, including Nigeria, to demand self-governance and greater political representation. This organization provided both a model and inspiration for the emerging nationalist activities in Nigeria.

question: The AU's right to intervene in a member country in crisis portrays it as
solution: The AU's right to intervene in a member country in crisis portrays it as an international organization.

question: An advantage of flexible constitution is that it allows for
solution: An advantage of flexible constitution is that it allows for ease of amendment. Changes can be made easily in order to accommodate changing situations in the society. Its amendment does not require or involve much time and financial cost.

question: The principle that advocates strict adherence to the fundamental laws of a country is
solution:
Constitutionalism is the principle that advocates strict adherence to fundamental laws of a country.

This is so when it is well noted that constitutionalism is defined as the principle which maintains that those who govern the society should do so in accordance with the laws of the land. It also means that those who rule the society should follow the stipulations of the constitution strictly.

question: A form of government in which the nobles rule for the benefit of all is
solution:
Aristocracy is a form of government in which the nobles rule for the benefit of all.

This form of government was advocated by some Greek philosophers like Aristotle and Plato who argued that true democracy will depend on the type of citizens a country with wise and just citizens likely to produce good citizens. For this reason, they believed that a country should be governed by the best citizens.

question: A prominent feature of a presidential system of government is
solution: In a presidential system of government, the three branches: executive, legislative, and judicial, are distinctly separate and operate independently of each other. This separation of powers is a hallmark of the presidential model, ensuring that no single branch becomes too dominant and that each branch can check and balance the others. This contrasts with parliamentary systems, where the executive is typically derived from the legislature and can be removed through a vote of no confidence.

question: Nigeria's commitment to the liberation of Africa could best be seen in her policy towards
solution: During the liberation struggles in Southern Africa, Nigeria was a leading voice in supporting anti-colonial and anti-apartheid movements. It provided both political and moral support to groups fighting apartheid in South Africa and to liberation movements in Angola. These policies were part of Nigeria’s broader commitment to Pan-Africanism and the liberation of oppressed peoples across the continent, making its stance toward South Africa and Angola a prominent example of this commitment.

question: The set of policies on the basis of which countries interact with one another is called
solution:
The set of policies on the basis of which countries interact with one another is called foreign policy.
It could also be defined as the sum total of decision formulated by the government of a country to manipulate the international environment to attain certain national interest or objective.

question: The main attributes of a state are

option.a: government, the police and the armed forces
option.d: the press, the legislature, the executive and the judiciary

solution:
The four essential attributes of a state are:

Population: A defined group of people within the state.

Territory: A recognized geographical area with borders.

Government: An organized system to enforce laws and administer policies.

Sovereignty: Supreme authority to govern without external interference.

question: A major function of Warrant Chiefs was to
solution: Under the colonial system of indirect rule in Nigeria, warrant chiefs were appointed by the British colonial authorities to serve as intermediaries between the colonial government and the indigenous communities. Their primary function was to manage local administration, including tax collection, maintenance of order, and implementation of colonial policies, thus ensuring that the colonial government’s interests were maintained at the local level.

question: A state that is ruled by an elected citizen is
solution: Republic is defined as a situation or process whereby the people democratically elect their leaders into public or political offices.

question: The main role of pressure groups is to

option.a: influence the legislature to make decision favourable to them
option.d: use all means, including bribery, to achieve their objectives

solution: The primary role of pressure groups is to advocate for specific policies or interests by influencing legislators and policymakers. They achieve this through lobbying, submitting proposals, organizing campaigns, and testifying at hearings. For example, environmental groups push for climate legislation, while trade unions advocate for labor rights.

question: An electoral process in which candidates are selected for elective offices by party members is
solution: It is only the party that can nominate (choose) an individual for a position or post. Furthermore, it an in-house affair where the party’s candidate is given a ticket to vote for a particular post.

question: Who was the political head of the Old Oyo Empire?
solution: This position is regarded as sacred and spirit being. So the Alafin is regarded as the spiritual head of the Oyo kingdom.

question: The Igbo political system was based on
solution: The Igbo pre-colonial political system was fundamentally decentralized and based on kinship structures, with the Umunna serving as the primary unit of political organization. The Umunna refers to a patrilineal kinship group or extended family unit, consisting of individuals who share a common male ancestor. Each Umunna was led by the eldest male, known as the Diokpala, who, along with other elders, played a significant role in decision-making and governance within the community. This structure ensured that authority was distributed among various family heads, fostering a republican and egalitarian society

question: France introduced the policy of assimilation in her colonies primarily to

option.a: teach them the art of leadership

solution: The French policy of assimilation was designed to transform the cultural identities of colonized peoples by encouraging them to adopt French language, customs, and laws. The underlying goal was to integrate colonial subjects into French society, effectively changing their traditional ways of life to mirror that of France. This approach was rooted in the belief that spreading French civilization would "civilize" the colonies, leading to the erosion of indigenous cultures and practices.

question: Before Nigeria became a republic, the highest body charged with the administration of justice was the
solution: Before Nigeria became a republic in 1963, the highest judicial authority was the Judicial Committee of the Privy Council in the United Kingdom. During this period, decisions from Nigeria's Federal Supreme Court could be appealed to the Privy Council, which served as the final court of appeal for British colonies and certain Commonwealth countries

question: In which of the following systems is the power of the component units more than that of the central government?
solution: In a confederal system, the component units (such as states or regions) possess more authority than the central government. This arrangement allows the constituent entities to retain their sovereignty and delegate only specific, limited powers to the central authority, often related to common interests like defense or foreign affairs. Consequently, the central government operates at the behest of the component units and lacks independent authority.

question: Under a presidential system of government, the legislature and the executive are

option.a: elected separately to an unfixed term
option.b: appointed at the same time to an unfixed term
option.c: appointed by the judiciary to a fixed term
option.d: elected separately to a fixed term

solution: In a presidential system of government, the executive and legislative branches are distinct and independent from each other. Both the president (executive) and the members of the legislature are elected separately by the populace. Additionally, they serve fixed terms in office, meaning their tenure is predetermined and not subject to arbitrary dismissal by the other branch. This structure ensures a clear separation of powers and a system of checks and balances between the branches.

question: A referendum is a device to ensure that

option.b: legislators vote to resolve contentious issues
option.c: decisions are effected with the consent of citizens
option.d: bye-elections are held to fill vacant positions

solution: A referendum is a direct voting process in which the electorate is invited to vote on a particular proposal and decide on issues such as constitutional amendments, changes in laws, or specific policies. This process ensures that decisions reflect the will of the citizens, as it requires their direct approval or disapproval. Unlike regular elections, which select representatives, referendums allow citizens to have a say on specific legislative or policy issues directly.

question: The Mid-Western Region was created in Nigeria in
solution: The Mid-Western Region was created in Nigeria on August 9, 1963. It was established as a result of the 1962 census and was the first new region to be created in Nigeria after independence. This change was part of a broader effort to accommodate Nigeria's diverse ethnic and cultural groups in the federal structure.

question: The council constituted by Lord Lugard in 1914 has
solution: The council constituted by Lord Lugard in 1914, following the amalgamation of Northern and Southern Nigeria, had 36 members; 24 official and 12 unofficial members. This was part of his administrative structure aimed at unifying the two regions.

question: Nigeria’s withdrawal from the Edinburgh Commonwealth Games in July 1986 was in protest against British

option.b: failure to impose sanctions on South Africa
option.d: support for UNITA rebels in Angola

solution: Nigeria withdrew from the 1986 Edinburgh Commonwealth Games in protest against the British government's reluctance to impose sanctions on apartheid South Africa.
